examples: Geomagnetic activity, the natural variations in Earth's magnetic field, it fluctuates in 11-year cycles.
Oh yes, and there's geomagnetic reversal.
In a infinite and empty space the geomagnetic field falls off in strength with the third power of distance.
One of the biggest geomagnetic storms on record was the Carrington event of 1859.
When a massive solar flare erupts on the sun, it can cause geomagnetic storms on the Earth.
